description:
Your role: You are a Site-Reliability Engineer who has just been handed an INI-style
    configuration file that lists several internal and external services.  
    Management wants an easily machine-readable “source-of-truth” JSON file that
    summarises every service in the INI file so other tooling can consume it.

    Starting situation  
    ├── The working directory is /home/user.  
    └── There is a sub-directory /home/user/sre_monitor/ with a single configuration
        file already present at the exact path  
        /home/user/sre_monitor/config/services.ini

    Goal  
    1. Parse /home/user/sre_monitor/config/services.ini.  
       • For every section, read the four keys exactly as written below:
         section-name (the literal section header), name, host, port.  
       • Ignore all other keys if they ever appear.  
    2. Create a new directory called /home/user/sre_monitor/output/ (if it does not
       yet exist).  
    3. Inside that directory, create a file called
       /home/user/sre_monitor/output/service_endpoints.json  containing a JSON
       array.  
       • The array MUST be ordered in the same order in which the sections appear in
         the INI file (top-to-bottom).  
       • Each array element MUST be an object with exactly four fields in the
         following order:  
           1. "section"   – string, the section header  
           2. "name"      – string, the value of the “name” key  
           3. "host"      – string, the value of the “host” key  
           4. "port"      – number, the value of the “port” key parsed as an integer  
       • The file must be valid, minified JSON (one single line, no trailing
         newline).

    Output format the grader will check
    The grader will open /home/user/sre_monitor/output/service_endpoints.json and
    expect to see one single line that looks exactly like the following (spacing,
    ordering, and capitalisation must match):

    [{"section":"web","name":"frontend","host":"127.0.0.1","port":80},{"section":"db","name":"database","host":"127.0.0.1","port":5432},{"section":"external_dns","name":"google_dns","host":"8.8.8.8","port":53}]

    Steps you will need (high level, do NOT paste these commands verbatim):  
    • Inspect the INI file to understand its layout.  
    • Use awk, grep/sed, python’s configparser, or any other CLI tools of your
      choice to extract the required keys.  
    • Build the JSON array in the exact order and write it to the target file.  
    • Confirm correctness by displaying the file contents back to the console.

    Verification hints  
    • The automated test will fail if the directory path or file name does not
      match exactly.  
    • The JSON must be a single line with no whitespace other than the literal
      spaces shown above (i.e., only inside the JSON string values).  
    • The key ordering inside each object is significant.

    When done, provide the absolute path of the created JSON file as final output.
truth:
Pre-existing file and its contents (already on disk when the task starts):

    Path: /home/user/sre_monitor/config/services.ini

    Contents (including blank lines):
    ----------------------------------
    [web]
    name = frontend
    host = 127.0.0.1
    port = 80

    [db]
    name = database
    host = 127.0.0.1
    port = 5432

    [external_dns]
    name = google_dns
    host = 8.8.8.8
    port = 53
    ----------------------------------

    Expected artefact after successful completion:

    1. Directory  
       /home/user/sre_monitor/output/   (permissions 755 or inherited)

    2. File  
       /home/user/sre_monitor/output/service_endpoints.json

       Exact file contents (single line, no trailing newline):
       [{"section":"web","name":"frontend","host":"127.0.0.1","port":80},{"section":"db","name":"database","host":"127.0.0.1","port":5432},{"section":"external_dns","name":"google_dns","host":"8.8.8.8","port":53}]
